div.p: RESULT: Memory Corruption Thread 1 loaded the pointer but hadn't protected it yet. Thread 2 freed it.
RESULT: Safe Access Validation detected that the pointer changed. Thread 1 retries with the current value, ensuring protection is valid before dereferencing.

We're not protecting a specific node — we're protecting "whatever is at source right now". If another thread changes source between our load and protect, validation detects this and we retry with the new value. The protection is only valid if our published value matches the current source value.

ol.li: validate: current = load(&top) current != node? → RETRY
Validation FAILED Retry loop... node = load(&top) new value
retire(old_node) scan() → sees protection CANNOT FREE
hp_protect(slot, new_node) validate: MATCH → SAFE TO ACCESS

g.text: |'md ← Vulnerable window between load and protect →'|
